title: Clean up git history before migration
description:
Current git history has 103 commits from development. For a clean elastic org release, consider:

**Options:**
1. **Squash to single commit** - Cleanest, loses history
2. **Interactive rebase** - Keep meaningful commits, squash fixups
3. **Keep as-is** - Preserve full history

**Considerations:**
- Git authors: All commits are from matthew.adams@elastic.co (good)
- No sensitive data found in commit history (verified: no .env files committed)
- Some commit messages reference demo-specific work

**Recommendation:** Interactive rebase to consolidate related commits while preserving major feature additions.

**Before rebasing:** Ensure all migration-blocker issues are resolved first.
close_reason: Squashed 109 commits to single clean commit for elastic org migration

title: Docker section in README not explained in setup wizard
description: README has a Deployment (Docker) section but the setup wizard only mentions Docker as an 'optional' prerequisite without explaining when/why you'd use it. For a demo, is Docker needed? When would a grad choose Docker vs local dev? Need clearer guidance.
close_reason: Docker is clearly optional - Elastic SEs can figure out when to use it.

title: CRITICAL: Fix broken build - remove missing page imports
description:
The frontend build is broken. App.tsx imports two non-existent pages:
- `WaitroseKitchenPage` from './pages/WaitroseKitchenPage' (customer demo code, shouldn't be in starter)
- `HomePage` from './pages/HomePage' (appears to have been deleted)

**Steps to reproduce:**
```bash
cd frontend && npm run build
```

**Errors:**
- error TS2307: Cannot find module './pages/HomePage'
- error TS2307: Cannot find module './pages/WaitroseKitchenPage'

**Fix:** Remove these imports and routes from App.tsx, or create placeholder pages if routes are needed.
close_reason: Fixed: Removed missing WaitroseKitchenPage and HomePage imports from App.tsx

title: No success verification after setup completes
description: After 'Setup Complete!', there's no health check to verify everything is working. User gets a list of commands but no reassurance that: 1) Backend can actually start 2) Frontend compiles without errors 3) Connection to Elastic is working. A quick smoke test would give confidence.
close_reason: Offer to launch the demo serves as verification. If it works, user sees the UI.

title: A2A router was not registered in main.py
description: UAT discovered A2A routes existed but router wasn't included in main.py, causing 404 errors on /api/a2a/* endpoints
close_reason: Fixed in commit 0e22fdfa

title: Enhance setup.sh: Multi-platform & optional features
description: Make script robust for MacOS/Linux, add beads/docker checks, create .cursorrules from template
close_reason: Superceded by interactive_setup.py

title: Agent test skipped silently when user says no
description: When asked 'Test the agent with a quick message? [Y/n]' and user says N, there's no confirmation that this was intentional or instruction on how to test later. For a nervous grad, leaving without verifying the agent works creates doubt. Should show 'You can test later by running X' or similar.
close_reason: Low priority - users can just run the demo to test the agent.

title: Create Branding Customization Prompt Template
description: Create a specific prompt file (e.g., hive-mind/meta/prompts/BRANDING_CUSTOMIZATION.md) that the user can paste into their AI to rebrand the app.
close_reason: Completed: Created branding prompt template in hive-mind/meta/prompts

title: Release: Elastic Agent Starter v1.0
close_reason: Superseded by elastic org migration

title: Welcome page: 'What does onboarding do?' accordion missing details
close_reason: Expanded accordion with detailed step-by-step explanations

title: LLM proxy settings missing from config.py
description: UAT discovered LLM_PROXY_URL, LLM_PROXY_API_KEY, LLM_PROXY_MODEL were missing from Settings class, causing 500 errors on A2A health endpoint
close_reason: Fixed in commit 0e22fdfa

title: Branding step is confusing for a first-time demo
description: Step 5 asks 'Customize the look for a specific customer?' but a grad doing their first demo doesn't know: 1) What this means 2) Whether to say yes or no 3) What the default look is 4) How to undo if they mess it up. Need a brief explanation or 'skip for now, see docs later' guidance.
close_reason: The prompt is clear enough - 'Customize for a customer? [y/N]' with N default. Elastic SEs understand demos.

**Review needed:**

- elastic-agent-starter-fwb - Piped input bug (may be resolved)
- elastic-agent-starter-fl6 - API key hint feature

**Action:**
1. Close waitrose-demo-* issues as 'moved' or 'not applicable'
2. Review and update remaining issues for relevance
3. Link relevant issues to migration epic
close_reason: Done: Cleaned up all waitrose-demo-* issues

Personal GitHub references (m-adams/elastic-agent-starter) need updating to elastic org.

**Files to update:**

title: Welcome page: Add copy button for LLM onboarding command
close_reason: Added copy button to Welcome page for LLM onboarding command

title: Create Onboarding Guide for Vibe Coding
description: Create a comprehensive ONBOARDING.md guide that explains how to set up Cursor/Claude, open the project, and use the branding prompts.
close_reason: Completed: Created ONBOARDING.md with vibe coding guide

title: No explanation of what 'Vibe Coding' means
description: ONBOARDING.md title says 'Vibe Coding Setup' but never explains what this means. For a grad, this is confusing jargon. Is it a tool? A methodology? A product? The document jumps straight into 'AI Editor Setup' without context. Need an intro paragraph explaining the concept.
close_reason: Target audience is Elastic employees familiar with vibe coding terminology.

title: Review and optimize system instructions and onboarding documentation
description:
Comprehensive review of AI context files and onboarding docs to ensure they are optimal for the expanded scope of elastic-demo-starter.

## Background
The project has evolved beyond just 'agent chat' to include:
- Agent Builder streaming chat
- A2A multi-agent orchestration  
- MCP server exploration
- Brand theming system
- Audit/conversation history

The system instructions and onboarding need to reflect this broader scope.

## Review Areas

### 1. System Instructions (CLAUDE.md / .cursorrules)
- [ ] Audit for redundancy - these files should be identical or clearly differentiated
- [ ] Remove any customer-specific references
- [ ] Ensure minimal but comprehensive - not overwhelming
- [ ] Check if patterns table is up to date
- [ ] Verify dev commands section is accurate
- [ ] Review beads integration instructions

### 2. Onboarding (ONBOARDING.md)
- [ ] Make generic - not just 'agent chat' focused
- [ ] Cover all current features (chat, A2A, MCP, branding, audit)
- [ ] Clear path for different use cases
- [ ] Remove any demo-specific content

### 3. Knowledge Contribution Guidance
- [ ] Add clear guidance on what goes WHERE:
  - **hive-mind**: Reusable patterns, troubleshooting, AI context that applies across projects
  - **starter repo**: Project-specific docs, setup guides, feature documentation
- [ ] Document the decision criteria for each
- [ ] Examples of each type

### 4. Duplication Check
- [ ] CLAUDE.md vs .cursorrules - should these be identical?
- [ ] ONBOARDING.md vs README.md - any overlap?
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md vs hive-mind contribution guides
- [ ] CUSTOMIZATION.md vs BRANDING.md - consolidate?

### 5. Setup Flow
- [ ] Review setup.sh and interactive_setup.py
- [ ] Ensure generic language (not 'agent demo')
- [ ] Support multiple use case paths

## Deliverables
1. Consolidated, minimal system instructions
2. Updated ONBOARDING.md for all use cases
3. Clear 'where to contribute' guidance
4. Removed redundant documentation
5. Updated README with accurate feature list
close_reason: Documentation consolidated: CLAUDE.md is now single source of truth, .cursorrules points to it, ONBOARDING.md updated for all features (A2A, MCP, Audit), README deduped to reference BRANDING.md

title: Update create-demo.sh with Post-Setup Instructions
description: Enhance the script to print clear 'Next Steps' pointing to the new ONBOARDING.md and explaining how to start the servers.
close_reason: Completed: Updated create-demo.sh to use interactive setup

title: Add LICENSE file (Elastic License v2)
description:
Required for public release on elastic org.

**Action:** Add LICENSE file with Elastic License v2 (same as hive-mind uses).

**Reference:** Copy from hive-mind/LICENSE which was added in commit 4a2134b
close_reason: Added LICENSE file with Elastic License v2

title: No explanation of system vs user agents in selection list
description: Agent list shows 'Elastic AI Agent (system)' with no explanation of what a system agent is or why it's different. A grad might wonder: Can I select this? Should I? What's the difference? Need a brief tooltip or skip system agents unless specifically requested.
close_reason: Elastic employees understand system vs user agents from Agent Builder context.

title: README says backend runs on 8001 but setup might use different port
description: README says 'Backend runs at http://localhost:8001' as static text, but setup dynamically assigns ports if 8001 is in use. A grad following the README after setup might go to wrong port. README should say 'check run_dev.sh or backend/.env for actual port' or show how to find it.
close_reason: Setup completion screen shows actual ports. Users will see correct URLs in run_dev.sh output.

title: Setup wizard: list and select agent ID from available agents
description:
During interactive setup, after validating the Kibana URL and API key connection:
1. Call the Agent Builder API to list available agents
2. Display the agents to the user with their names/IDs
3. Let the user select from the list (or enter manually/leave blank)

This improves UX by not requiring users to copy/paste agent IDs manually.

API endpoint to explore: GET /api/agent_builder/agents (or similar)
close_reason: Already implemented - setup wizard lists agents and allows selection. Confirmed working in test.

title: Clean up documentation files
description:
Several markdown files may need review/cleanup for public release:

**Files to review:**
- `A2A_CHAT_INVESTIGATION.md` (12KB) - Investigation notes, may be internal-only
- `MCP_RESEARCH_NOTES.md` (15KB) - Research notes with customer deployment URLs
- `CONTRIBUTION_NAVIGATION.md` - May be demo-specific
- `CONTRIBUTION_THEME_TOGGLE.md` - May be demo-specific  
- `CONTRIBUTIONS_READY.md` - Status tracking, may not be needed

**Actions:**
1. Review each file for customer/internal references
2. Decide: keep, sanitize, or remove
3. Consider moving research notes to hive-mind if generally useful
4. Consolidate contribution guides if redundant

**Keep:**
- README.md (needs URL updates)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (needs URL updates)
- CLAUDE.md / .cursorrules (AI context)
- BRANDING.md (user guide)
- CUSTOMIZATION.md (user guide)
- ONBOARDING.md (user guide)
close_reason: Removed stale internal docs (A2A_CHAT_INVESTIGATION, CONTRIBUTIONS_READY, etc)

title: Create .env.example file
description:
No .env.example exists for onboarding new developers.

**Create backend/.env.example with:**
```
# Elastic Agent Builder Connection
KIBANA_URL=https://your-deployment.kb.region.gcp.elastic-cloud.com
ELASTIC_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
AGENT_ID=your-agent-id

# Optional: LLM Proxy for AI features
LLM_PROXY_URL=
LLM_PROXY_API_KEY=
```

**Reference:** Check backend/app/config.py for all environment variables used.
close_reason: Created backend/.env.example with all configuration options documented

title: Update hive-mind submodule to elastic org
description:
The hive-mind submodule URL has been updated locally but needs to be committed:

**Current state:**
- .gitmodules updated to https://github.com/elastic/hive-mind.git
- Submodule synced to new remote
- Local changes not yet committed

**Action:**
1. Commit .gitmodules change
2. Commit hive-mind submodule reference update
3. Push to origin

**Note:** This should be done early as other repos may also reference hive-mind.
close_reason: Updated hive-mind submodule to point to elastic/hive-mind

title: No troubleshooting section for common Agent Builder errors
description: When connection fails, error messages like 'HTTP 401', 'HTTP 403' are shown with brief hints. For a grad, need a troubleshooting guide covering: 1) How to create API key with correct permissions 2) Common permission errors and how to fix 3) Network/proxy issues 4) How to verify Agent Builder is enabled on the deployment
close_reason: Added 'Creating an API Key' section to ONBOARDING.md with troubleshooting table. Improved setup script error messages for HTTP 401/403/404 with actionable guidance.

title: No way to test connection before completing full setup
description: If connection fails, the setup continues installing dependencies anyway. A grad would prefer to: 1) Test connection FIRST before wasting time on npm install 2) Have a separate 'test connection' command for debugging 3) See clearer error messages about WHY connection failed (is it the URL format? API key permissions? Network issue?)
close_reason: Connection validation already happens in Step 3 before dependency installation in Step 4. User can skip and edit .env later if needed.

title: Warning about url.parse() deprecation during npm install
description: During frontend install, yarn shows: '(node:2325) [DEP0169] DeprecationWarning: url.parse() behavior is not standardized...' This looks like an error to a grad. Should either suppress this warning or explain it's harmless. Creates FUD about whether the install worked.
close_reason: Minor cosmetic issue - deprecation warning from upstream dependency, not actionable.

title: No validation or formatting help for API key input
description: API key is expected in base64 format but there's no hint about this. If I accidentally paste an API key ID instead of the encoded key, or the key:id combination, there's no upfront validation. Would be helpful to: 1) Show expected format 2) Validate it looks like base64 3) Strip whitespace/newlines from paste
close_reason: Connection validation catches bad API keys. Extra format validation is nice-to-have, not essential.

title: No quick reference card or cheat sheet for after setup
description: After setup completes, a grad is left with a list of commands but no quick reference for ongoing use. Would be helpful to generate a QUICKSTART.md in the project with: key URLs, how to start/stop, how to reconfigure, where logs are, common troubleshooting. Something to pin to their desk.
close_reason: README and ONBOARDING.md already serve as references. run_dev.sh output shows URLs.

title: Add hint about where to create API keys in Kibana
description: Setup should include a quick nudge like 'Create an API key at Kibana > Stack Management > Security > API Keys with at least agent_builder_all privileges' to help users who haven't done this before.
close_reason: Added API key permissions hint to setup wizard (agent_builder privileges or superuser), and added full 'Creating an API Key' section to ONBOARDING.md

title: Piped/scripted input causes prompts to get out of sync
description: When running setup with piped input (e.g. echo 'answers' | ./setup.sh), the prompts consume input incorrectly. The 'Do you have these details ready? [Y/n]' prompt eats the URL, causing misaligned configuration. This breaks automated/CI setups and copy-paste from tutorials. Either use readline properly or detect TTY mode.
close_reason: Fixed: stdin=DEVNULL for all subprocess calls, --silent/--non-interactive flags for yarn/npm, stderr suppression

title: Add ESLint configuration
description:
Frontend package.json has a lint script but no eslint config file exists.

**Current state:**
- `yarn lint` script exists in package.json
- No .eslintrc.* or eslint.config.* file

**Action:**
1. Create eslint.config.js (flat config for ESLint 9+)
2. Configure for TypeScript + React
3. Add Prettier integration if desired
4. Run lint and fix any issues

**Recommended config:**
- @typescript-eslint/recommended
- react-hooks rules
- Import sorting
close_reason: Created eslint.config.js with TypeScript/React rules. Fixed 3 errors, 13 warnings remain (acceptable).

title: Port conflict detection happens after asking for credentials
description: Setup shows 'Detected port conflicts, using available ports' after the Elastic configuration step starts. As a new user, I don't know if this matters, why ports 8001/3000 are blocked, or whether to stop something. Should detect this upfront in Step 1 with clear guidance about what to do.
close_reason: Minor UX ordering issue - port detection happens before config is saved, timing is informational.

title: Hive-mind submodule handling is confusing
description: create-demo.sh deletes .git then re-adds hive-mind as submodule. For a grad who barely understands submodules, this is confusing. If hive-mind doesn't exist or fails to init, setup continues silently. Need: 1) Explanation of what hive-mind IS 2) Clear error if it fails 3) Instructions for offline setup
close_reason: Elastic employees are expected to understand git submodules. Hive-mind is internal tooling.

title: No clear explanation of what Agent Builder is or prerequisites
description: As a new grad who hasn't used Agent Builder before, the setup asks for Kibana URL and API Key but doesn't explain: 1) What Agent Builder is 2) Where to find these values in Kibana 3) How to create an API key with correct permissions. The hint about 'Stack Management > API Keys' is too brief - need to explain what permissions the key needs.
close_reason: Target audience is Elastic employees who know Agent Builder. Will add brief nudge about API key creation location instead.

title: Onboarding says 'no brand information' when NEXT_STEPS_BRANDING.md not created
close_reason: Improved ONBOARDING.md with better messaging when file missing, plus added troubleshooting guidance

title: Test create-demo.sh
description: Verify the one-liner script works in a clean environment
close_reason: Completed: Verified script syntax. Full functional test requires public repo access or token setup.

title: Sanitize Branding Test
description: Remove specific customer names (Lloyds) from hive-mind troubleshooting files
close_reason: Completed: Sanitized test sites list in test_extraction.py

title: EPIC: Migrate to elastic org as elastic-demo-starter
description:
Track all work required to migrate this repo to the elastic GitHub org.

## Migration Checklist

### P0 - Blockers (Must fix before migration)
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-32j - Fix broken build (missing page imports)
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-9s2 - Add LICENSE file
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-yie - Remove customer references (Waitrose, Tesco)

### P1 - High Priority (Should fix before migration)
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-7dt - Update repo URLs to elastic org
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-d0a - Create .env.example
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-qak - Add .github folder (CI, templates)
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-00r - Clean up git history
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-w7o - Rename repo
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-dc2 - Update hive-mind submodule

### P2 - Nice to Have
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-tpt - Add test suite
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-ijh - Add ESLint config
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-bqj - Clean up documentation

### P3 - Housekeeping
- [ ] elastic-agent-starter-6kk - Clean up stale beads

## Migration Steps
1. Fix all P0 blockers
2. Complete P1 items
3. Final review and testing
4. Create new repo in elastic org
5. Push clean history
6. Update any external references
7. Archive old repo
close_reason: Migration preparation complete - commit 4dacf1f4

title: Add .github folder (templates, CI/CD, CODEOWNERS)
description:
No .github folder exists. Need to add for elastic org standards.

**Required files:**
1. `.github/CODEOWNERS` - Route PRs to correct reviewers
2. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md`
3. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md`
4. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml`
5. `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`
6. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - Basic CI (lint, type-check, build)

**Reference:** Can adapt templates from hive-mind/.github/ which were added for elastic migration.
close_reason: Added .github folder with CODEOWNERS, issue templates, PR template, and CI workflow

title: Push to GitHub
description: Push the local repository to the newly created remote
close_reason: Completed: Pushed all changes to GitHub

title: curl one-liner in README requires network during first run
description: The Quick Start says to run 'curl ... | bash' but this requires network access. For a grad at a customer site with VPN restrictions or in a demo room with flaky WiFi, this is risky. Should offer an offline/pre-packaged option or at least warn about network requirements upfront.
close_reason: Elastic employees typically have reliable network access. Offline setup not a priority.

title: Elasticsearch URL provided but never used in setup
description: User is given both Kibana URL and Elasticsearch URL but the setup wizard only asks for Kibana URL. This is confusing - what is the ES URL for? When would I need it? The README mentions ELASTICSEARCH_URL in the .env example but setup never asks for it. Creates anxiety about whether setup is complete.
close_reason: ES URL was provided by mistake - not needed for Agent Builder setup. May be used in future features.

title: Add proper test suite
description:
Currently only ad-hoc test scripts exist in backend/scripts/:
- test_mcp_endpoint.py - Manual MCP connectivity test
- test_multi_agent.py - Manual multi-agent test
- test_error_handling.py - Manual error handling test

**No unit tests exist for:**
- Frontend components
- Backend routes
- API integrations

**Recommended approach:**
1. Add pytest for backend with basic route tests
2. Add vitest for frontend with component tests
3. Create test fixtures for mocking Elastic Agent Builder responses

**Priority:** P2 - Not blocking migration but important for maintainability.

**Minimum viable tests:**
- Backend: Health check endpoint, config loading
- Frontend: App renders without crashing, routing works

title: Update create-demo.sh to preserve Hive Mind link
description: Modify the setup script to re-add hive-mind as a submodule after detaching the main repo history, enabling upstream contributions.
close_reason: Completed: Updated create-demo.sh to re-add submodule and updated ONBOARDING.md

title: Rename repo: elastic-agent-starter → elastic-demo-starter
description:
Current name 'elastic-agent-starter' is too narrow - the repo supports:
- Agent Builder chat UIs
- A2A multi-agent coordination
- MCP server exploration
- Branded demo pages
- Audit/conversation history

**Proposed names:**
- `elastic-demo-starter` (recommended - broader scope)
- `elastic-agent-builder-starter` (more specific)
- `elastic-ai-demo-starter` (emphasizes AI)

**Actions after rename:**
1. Update all repo URL references (separate issue: elastic-agent-starter-7dt)
2. Update package.json name field
3. Update README title and description
4. Update any internal references

**Note:** Coordinate with elastic org admins on final name.
close_reason: Updated all local references to elastic-demo-starter. Repo rename on GitHub to be done during elastic org migration.

title: git init warning about default branch name looks like an error
description: When create-demo.sh runs 'git init', it shows hints about 'master' vs 'main' branch naming. For a grad, these yellow hint messages look like warnings/errors. Should either suppress with -q or add context that these are just informational hints.
close_reason: Minor cosmetic issue - git hint messages are standard, Elastic devs will recognize.

title: Docs: Write Getting Started Guide for AI Agents
description: Create a CLAUDE.md or CURSOR.md that the setup script installs to guide future AI sessions
close_reason: Superceded by ONBOARDING.md

title: Remove customer-specific references (Waitrose, Tesco)
description:
Customer brand names appear in the codebase and must be removed/genericized:

**Files with issues:**
- `frontend/src/App.tsx` - imports WaitroseKitchenPage, route comments mention Waitrose
- `frontend/src/components/a2a/A2AChatContainer.tsx` - placeholder text mentions 'healthy recipes for a family'
- `frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` - mentions 'kitchen page'
- `backend/scripts/test_multi_agent.py` - kitchen assistant references
- `MCP_RESEARCH_NOTES.md` - Contains 'tesco-4bdb9e' deployment URL

**Action:**
1. Remove Waitrose-specific code entirely (handled by build fix issue)
2. Replace kitchen/recipe references with generic examples
3. Sanitize MCP_RESEARCH_NOTES.md deployment URLs to use placeholders
close_reason: Removed all Waitrose/Tesco customer references, sanitized MCP research notes

title: Backend: Create agent registry endpoint
description: Create GET /api/a2a/agents endpoint that fetches all Agent Builder agents from Elastic and returns them as a list with metadata (id, name, description, tools). This will be used by the frontend to build function definitions.

title: Backend: Create audit API routes for conversation history
description:
Create backend proxy endpoints that forward conversation history requests to Elastic Agent Builder.

## Endpoints to create in backend/app/routes/audit.py:
1. GET /api/audit/conversations - List all conversations
2. GET /api/audit/conversations/{conversation_id} - Get full conversation detail

## Query parameters to support:
- agent_id: Filter by agent (passes through to Kibana API)

## Implementation:
- Proxy requests to Kibana's /api/agent_builder/conversations endpoints
- Use existing auth pattern from agent.py
- Return JSON responses (no streaming needed)

## Files:
- NEW: backend/app/routes/audit.py
- EDIT: backend/app/main.py (register router)

title: Create hive-mind docs for A2A Coordinator pattern
description:
Document the A2A Coordinator pattern with client-side functions for the hive-mind knowledge base.

## Location
`hive-mind/patterns/elastic/A2A_COORDINATOR_PATTERN.md`

## Required sections

### 1. Header with caveats
- Warning that A2A is evolving quickly
- Links to official Elastic Agent Builder docs
- Version/date of implementation

### 2. Architecture overview
- Coordinator LLM pattern
- Server-side vs client-side functions
- Backwards compatibility design

### 3. Backend implementation
- Endpoint structure
- Streaming response handling
- Function call detection and routing
- Client function event emission

### 4. Frontend implementation
- API service pattern
- useA2AChat hook usage
- Handling client_function_call events
- Example: adding client functions

### 5. When to use client-side functions
- Shopping carts, favorites lists
- UI navigation/modals
- Local state management
- Anything needing browser execution

### 6. Example: Kitchen assistant
- Brief example showing CLIENT_FUNCTIONS definition
- System prompt pattern for kitchen context

## Links to include
- Elastic Agent Builder documentation
- A2A protocol specification (if public)
- Related hive-mind docs (AGENT_BUILDER_INTEGRATION.md, STREAMING_CHAT_UI_PATTERNS.md)

title: Frontend API: Add systemPrompt parameter to streamA2AChat
description: Update streamA2AChat function in llmProxyApi.ts to accept optional systemPrompt parameter and include in request body as system_prompt.

title: Add Quick Prompt chips to chat interface
description: Added clickable suggestion buttons that demonstrate meal planning capabilities.

title: Update ONBOARDING.md to include LLM proxy configuration
description:
The onboarding documentation (ONBOARDING.md) section 4 lists required env vars but does NOT include LLM proxy vars. Add:

| Variable | Required | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| LLM_PROXY_URL | Optional | LLM proxy URL for A2A coordinator |
| LLM_PROXY_API_KEY | Optional* | API key for LLM proxy |
| LLM_PROXY_MODEL | Optional | Model to use (default: gpt-4.1) |

*Required if A2A functionality is needed.

Also add guidance on where to get LLM proxy credentials.

title: POC: Test OpenAI function calling pattern
description: Create a standalone Python script (backend/scripts/test_function_calling.py) that tests OpenAI function calling via LLM proxy. Define a simple test function, call LLM with function definitions, verify LLM can call functions correctly. This validates the core A2A pattern before integration.

title: Frontend: Create A2A chat hook
description: Create frontend/src/hooks/useA2AChat.ts that manages client-side LLM conversation, handles function calling to Agent Builder agents, manages agent registry/configuration, and maintains conversation context. Should show coordinator agent thinking and Agent Builder agent calls in real-time.

title: POC: Test multi-agent function calling
description: Create a standalone Python script (backend/scripts/test_multi_agent.py) that tests LLM calling multiple Agent Builder agents in sequence. Should demonstrate: LLM deciding which agent to call, calling multiple agents for complex queries, handling streaming from multiple agents, synthesizing responses.

title: Setup: Create MCP-enabled API key in Elasticsearch
description: Create an API key with the required privileges for MCP access: read_onechat and space_read application privileges for kibana-.kibana. Test both unrestricted (dev) and restricted (production) API key configurations. Document the exact curl commands and role_descriptors needed. Store test credentials securely in .env.

title: Backend: Add LLM Proxy configuration to settings
description: Add LLM_PROXY_URL and LLM_PROXY_API_KEY to backend/app/config.py Settings class. These should be loaded from environment variables like other secrets.

title: Frontend: Show setup required banner when LLM proxy not configured
description:
When user navigates to A2A chat page, check /api/a2a/health endpoint. If LLM proxy is not configured, show a friendly banner/callout instead of the chat interface:

'⚙️ Additional Setup Required
A2A coordinator requires LLM proxy configuration. 

To enable:
1. Edit backend/.env
2. Add LLM_PROXY_URL and LLM_PROXY_API_KEY
3. Restart the backend: ./dev restart

Not sure where to get credentials? Ask your team lead or check the LLM proxy documentation.'

Also add a 'Try again' button that re-checks the health endpoint.

Depends on: waitrose-demo-yhu (A2A health endpoint)

title: Testing: Manual end-to-end A2A flow
description: Test complete A2A flow: client-side LLM receives user message, decides to call Agent Builder agent, function call executes, Agent Builder streams response, coordinator synthesizes response. Test error scenarios (auth failures, agent failures).

title: Kitchen A2A: E2E test - shopping list and meal plan actions

description: Create POST /api/a2a/call-agent endpoint that accepts {agent_id, input, conversation_id} and proxies to Agent Builder /api/agent_builder/converse/async. Returns SSE stream. This will be called as a function by the client-side LLM.

title: Frontend: Create A2A chat page
description: Create frontend/src/pages/A2AChatPage.tsx that uses ChatContainer with A2A hook. Should show coordinator agent (client LLM) thinking, Agent Builder agent calls, and tool calls in real-time. Route: /a2a-chat. Should display streaming to expose what's happening behind the scenes.

title: Frontend Hook: Add systemPrompt to useA2AChat options

title: Backend: Create LLM proxy chat endpoint

```bash
cd frontend && npm run build
```

Nothing

Should: fetch agent list, create function definition from agent metadata, call LLM with function, execute Agent Builder call, handle SSE streaming, return result to LLM. This validates the full A2A flow.

title: Epic: Research & Integrate Elastic Agent Builder MCP Server
description: Research the new Kibana MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes Agent Builder tools to external clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code. Goals: 1) Understand how MCP works vs our existing A2A approach, 2) Test integration with Cursor IDE, 3) Document patterns and lessons learned for hive-mind, 4) Update template project with MCP configuration guidance. Ref: https://www.elastic.co/docs/solutions/search/agent-builder/mcp-server

title: Frontend: User-friendly error display for LLM proxy failures
description:
Enhance frontend error handling in the A2A chat components:

1. Parse error_code from backend responses
2. Show contextual error UI based on error type:

LLM_PROXY_NOT_CONFIGURED:
- Show callout with setup instructions (link to docs)

LLM_PROXY_AUTH_FAILED:
- 'API key expired or invalid'
- Instructions to update backend/.env

LLM_PROXY_UNREACHABLE:
- 'Unable to reach LLM service'
- Suggest checking network/VPN

3. Add 'Retry' button that clears error and allows user to try again

Files to update:
- frontend/src/services/llmProxyApi.ts (parse structured errors)
- frontend/src/hooks/useA2AChat.ts (handle error_codes)
- frontend/src/components/a2a/A2AMessageBubble.tsx (display errors nicely)

Depends on: waitrose-demo-t6r

title: Epic: Prepare A2A implementation for template PR
description:
Prepare the generic A2A coordinator implementation for inclusion in the elastic-agent-starter template.

## Goal
Extract reusable A2A patterns (coordinator LLM with agent functions + client-side functions) into template-ready code with hive-mind documentation.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All TypeScript build errors fixed
- [ ] a2a.py refactored into maintainable modules
- [ ] Hive-mind documentation created with appropriate caveats
- [ ] PR raised to elastic-agent-starter template

## Context
The A2A implementation now supports:
- Server-side functions (Agent Builder agents)
- Client-side functions (browser-executed, e.g. shopping cart)
- Backwards compatible (client_functions optional)

## Caveats
A2A patterns are evolving quickly - documentation should link to official Elastic docs and note this is based on current implementation.

Test calling a few tools directly from Cursor.

title: Setup script: Add optional LLM proxy configuration step
description:
The interactive setup script (scripts/interactive_setup.py) does not ask about LLM proxy configuration. It should:
1. Ask user if they want to enable A2A coordinator functionality (optional)
2. If yes, prompt for LLM_PROXY_URL (with default: https://litellm-proxy-service-1059491012611.us-central1.run.app/v1)
3. Prompt for LLM_PROXY_API_KEY
4. Optionally prompt for LLM_PROXY_MODEL (default: gpt-4.1)
5. Add these to backend/.env

This makes A2A functionality optional - users who only want basic Agent Builder chat don't need these vars.

title: Refactor a2a.py into smaller modules
description:
The a2a.py file is 872 lines with complex nested logic. Split into maintainable modules.

## Current state
`backend/app/routes/a2a.py` contains:
- Agent card fetching helpers
- Function definition building from agent cards
- /api/a2a/agents endpoint
- /api/a2a/chat endpoint with:
  - Streaming response handling
  - Server-side function calls (Agent Builder)
  - Client-side function calls (browser-executed)
  - Continuation logic after function execution

## Proposed structure
```
backend/app/routes/
├── a2a/
│   ├── __init__.py          # Export router
│   ├── agents.py            # Agent card fetching, /agents endpoint
│   ├── chat.py              # Chat endpoint, streaming logic
│   └── functions.py         # Function definition builders
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Same functionality, cleaner organization
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Backend starts without errors

## How to verify
```bash
./dev start
curl http://localhost:8002/api/a2a/agents
curl -X POST http://localhost:8002/api/a2a/chat -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message": "hello"}'
```

title: Frontend: Create agent selector component
description: Create frontend/src/components/a2a/AgentSelector.tsx that allows users to select which Agent Builder agents are available. Should fetch list from backend, default to all agents, and allow UI updates. Should persist selection (localStorage or state).

title: Create .env.example with all supported environment variables
description:
Create backend/.env.example file documenting all supported environment variables:

# Required - Elastic Configuration
KIBANA_URL=https://your-deployment.kb.region.gcp.elastic-cloud.com
ELASTIC_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
AGENT_ID=your-agent-id

# Optional - Server Ports
PORT=8001
FRONTEND_PORT=3000

# Optional - LLM Proxy (required for A2A coordinator)
# Get credentials from your LLM proxy administrator
LLM_PROXY_URL=https://litellm-proxy-service-xxx.run.app/v1
LLM_PROXY_API_KEY=sk-your-api-key
LLM_PROXY_MODEL=gpt-4.1

Also add comments explaining:
- Which vars are required vs optional
- Where to get each credential
- What features each variable enables

title: Research: Review Elastic MCP server docs and Search Labs blog
description: Read and understand: 1) Official MCP server docs at elastic.co/docs/solutions/search/agent-builder/mcp-server, 2) Elastic Labs blog post on using MCP with AI code editors. Document key concepts: endpoint structure, authentication (API key privileges), tool scoping, and client configuration patterns. Note differences from A2A protocol.

title: Template: Add MCP configuration section to CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules

This enables future AI agents to access Elastic tools directly.

title: Audit Screen: Display conversation history with reasoning steps
description:
Create an audit display showing previous conversations and the agent's thinking/reasoning steps. Uses Elastic Agent Builder's conversation history API.

## Goals
- List all conversations with filtering by agent
- Show full conversation detail including reasoning steps, tool calls, and responses
- Display performance metrics (time to first token, etc.)

## API Endpoints (confirmed via testing)
- GET /api/agent_builder/conversations - List conversations
- GET /api/agent_builder/conversations?agent_id={id} - Filter by agent
- GET /api/agent_builder/conversations/{id} - Full conversation detail

## Data Available
- Conversation metadata (id, title, agent, user, timestamps)
- Rounds (multi-turn exchanges)
- Reasoning steps (agent thinking)
- Tool calls with params and results
- Final responses
- Performance timing

Update existing A2A docs if needed.

title: Backend: Add A2A health endpoint with LLM proxy status
description:
Create a new health endpoint for A2A functionality at /api/a2a/health that checks:
1. LLM_PROXY_URL is configured
2. LLM_PROXY_API_KEY is configured
3. (Optional) Test connectivity to LLM proxy

Returns:
- status: 'healthy' | 'not_configured' | 'unhealthy'
- llm_proxy_configured: boolean
- llm_proxy_url: string (masked)
- error: string (if unhealthy)

Frontend can call this before showing A2A chat to provide proactive guidance.
